# Scoring Rubric

The scoring rubric defines quality dimensions for evaluating outputs. Your review commands score each output against these dimensions to determine production readiness.

## Scoring Scale

| Score | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| 1 | Unacceptable — fails to meet the dimension's criteria |
| 2 | Below standard — significant gaps or issues |
| 3 | Acceptable — meets minimum requirements |
| 4 | Good — meets requirements with notable quality |
| 5 | Excellent — exceeds requirements, exemplary quality |

## Passing Threshold

**Minimum aggregate score**: ___ (define your threshold)

An output passes when the average score across all dimensions meets or exceeds this threshold. No individual dimension may score below ___ (define your floor).

---

## Example Dimensions

_These three examples show the structure. Replace them with your domain-specific quality dimensions._

### Dimension: Voice Consistency

**What it measures**: How well the output reflects the VoiceProfile attributes (perspective, tone, sentence patterns).

| Score | Criteria |
|-------|----------|
| 1 | Voice is unrecognizable — wrong perspective, inconsistent tone |
| 2 | Voice is partially present but drifts significantly in sections |
| 3 | Voice is mostly consistent with minor deviations |
| 4 | Voice is consistent throughout with appropriate variation |
| 5 | Voice is distinctive and compelling — unmistakably on-brand |

### Dimension: Content Relevance

**What it measures**: How well the output selects and incorporates relevant master content and narratives for the target context.

| Score | Criteria |
|-------|----------|
| 1 | Content is generic or irrelevant to the target |
| 2 | Some relevant content included but key material is missing |
| 3 | Relevant content is present and appropriately used |
| 4 | Content selection is strategic — strongest material for this target |
| 5 | Content selection demonstrates deep understanding of target needs |

### Dimension: Structural Compliance

**What it measures**: How well the output follows the StyleGuide's structural and formatting rules.

| Score | Criteria |
|-------|----------|
| 1 | Structure is absent or violates style guide rules |
| 2 | Structure is partially compliant with notable violations |
| 3 | Structure follows style guide with minor deviations |
| 4 | Structure is fully compliant and enhances readability |
| 5 | Structure is exemplary — demonstrates mastery of the format |

---
